brixs.PhotonEvents

class brixs.PhotonEvents(*args, **kwargs)

Returns a Photon events object.

Parameters:

  • x (array) – vector with x-coordinate of photon events.

  • y (array) – vector with y-coordinate of photon events.

  • filepath (string or path object, optional) – filepath.

How to initialize a Photon events object:

Empty

>>> pe = br.PhotonEvents()
From array
>>> pe = br.PhotonEvents(x, y)
>>> pe = br.PhotonEvents(x=x, y=y)

where x and y are 1D array or list.

From file
>>> pe = br.PhotonEvents(<filepath>)
>>> pe = br.PhotonEvents(filepath=<filepath>)

where filepath must point to a xy-type file, where comments must be marked with # and columns must be separated by , (comma).

x

vector with x-coordinate of photon events.

Type:

array

y

vector with y-coordinate of photon events.

Type:

array

filepath

filepath associated with data.

Type:

str or pathlib.Path

xlim, ylim

two element tuple with min and max x and y coordinates.

Type:

tuple

Computed (read-only) attributes:
data (array)

2 column data (x, y)

calculated_shift (list)

Calculated shifts.

Write-only attributes:

None

__init__(*args, **kwargs)

Initialize the object instance.

Raises:

AttributeError – if kwargs and args cannot be read.

Methods

__init__(*args, **kwargs)

Initialize the object instance.

binning(*args, **kwargs)

Compute the 2D histogram of the data (binning of the data).

calculate_shift(nbins[, deg, axis, limit_size])

Calculate intensity misalignments via cross-correlation.

calculate_spectrum(nbins[, axis])

Integrate data in one direction (sum columns or rows).

copy(*args, **kwargs)

Return a copy of the object.

fix_curvature(nbins[, deg, axis, limit_size])

Shift datapoints along a given axis to fix curvature via cc.

load(filepath[, only_data, verbose])

Load data from a text file.

plot([ax, set_limits])

Display data as an image.

save([filepath, only_data, check_overwrite, ...])

Save data to a text file.

set_shift([value, p, f, axis])

Shift photon events along a given axis.
